name: Tva Mid-South
description_short: TVA Mid-South Federal Credit Union is a Memphis-based credit union established in 1964, serving over 200 members in the communications and utilities sectors with checking, savings, and loan products.
description_long:
TVA Mid-South Federal Credit Union was established in 1964 and is headquartered in Memphis, Tennessee. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a not-for-profit financial institution serving a specialized membership base in the communications and utilities industries. The credit union has maintained steady operations for nearly six decades with a focus on industry-specific financial needs.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of banking and lending products including checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, share certificates (CDs), auto loans for new and used vehicles, personal loans, mortgage loans, wire transfers, online bill pay, online loan payments, and financial counseling services. Members also have access to financial calculators and online banking with mobile app functionality available on both iOS and Android platforms.

TVA Mid-South distinguishes itself through its not-for-profit structure, which allows it to offer competitive interest rates and lower fees compared to for-profit banks. The credit union maintains industry-specific membership eligibility focused on communications and utilities sectors, creating a tight-knit community of members with shared professional backgrounds. Current rates include 4.92% APR on new vehicle loans and 5.94% APR on used vehicle loans, positioning it competitively in the market.

However, prospective members should note that this is a very small institution with only one physical branch, no ATMs, and just over 200 members. The lobby is currently closed according to the website. The limited physical footprint and minimal assets ($3.6 million) mean this credit union serves an extremely niche membership base and may have limited capacity for growth or service expansion. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking accounts (Share Draft accounts with 16+ members)
pros: Not-for-profit structure offering better rates and lower fees than for-profit banks
Competitive auto loan rates (4.92% for new vehicles, 5.94% for used vehicles)
Comprehensive product suite including mortgages, personal loans, auto loans, and savings accounts
Mobile banking app available for iOS and Android devices
Established 60-year history demonstrating institutional stability since 1964
Free financial counseling services for members
Online bill pay and loan payment options for member convenience
cons: Only 1 physical branch with no ATM network, limiting accessibility
Extremely small institution with only 200+ members and $3.6 million in assets
Lobby currently closed according to website status
Restricted membership eligibility limited to communications and utilities sector employees
Limited service capacity and growth potential due to micro-institution size
best_for: Employees in the TVA, telecommunications, and utilities sectors seeking industry-focused banking
Members seeking a tight-knit community credit union with personal touch over large bank services
Individuals prioritizing lower fees and better rates through not-for-profit structure
Borrowers needing auto loans or personal loans with competitive rates in the Memphis area
diagnosis: TVA Mid-South is best for employees in the communications and utilities sectors seeking a specialized, community-focused credit union with competitive rates and personalized service. The primary caveat is the severe physical limitation—with only one branch, no ATMs, and a closed lobby, this credit union is only practical for members who can conduct business primarily online or by phone and have no need for walk-in branch access.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
